channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian Grand Prix 2026: Key Developments and Race Updates in Melbourne
item.description: The 2026 Australian Grand Prix saw an eventful start to the Formula 1 season, as unprecedented rule changes were tested on the Melbourne circuit. George Russell secured pole position, with Lewis Hamil...

item.title: Winnipeg Jets defeat Vancouver Canucks 3-2 in overtime with Morrissey’s game-winning goal
item.description: In a closely contested matchup at the Bell MTS Place, the Winnipeg Jets managed to edge past the Vancouver Canucks with a 3-2 victory in overtime. The decisive goal was scored by defenceman Josh Morri...

item.title: US Requests Sri Lanka to Keep Iranian Crew and Survivors Amid Naval Conflict
item.description: The United States has formally urged the Sri Lankan government not to repatriate survivors from the Iranian warship IRIS Dena, which it sank in the Indian Ocean near Galle, as well as the crew of a se...

item.title: Senior Kenya Kwanza figure linked to alleged illegal foreign recruitment network
item.description: The Nation Africa article reports that a senior Kenya Kwanza government official is being linked to a suspected illegal foreign recruitment racket that has drawn national attention. The story says int...

item.title: Public hearings underway in Johannesburg to probe possible political interference in TRC prosecutions
item.description: The Khampepe Commission of Inquiry has commenced public hearings in Newtown, Johannesburg, as of Friday, 6 March 2026. The purpose of this inquiry is to investigate whether political interference play...

item.title: Planning Commission Warns of Economic Risks to Pakistan from Escalating Middle East Conflict and Strait of Hormuz Disruptions
item.description: The Planning Commission of Pakistan has issued a warning in its Monthly Development Update for March 2026 about the serious economic challenges arising from the escalating conflict in the Middle East,...

item.title: Delhi woman survives attempted assault while travelling alone in Uttarakhand forest
item.description: A woman from Delhi, while travelling alone in Uttarakhand, reportedly had a frightening experience when someone tried to assault her. To protect herself, she spent the night hiding in a forest until s...

item.title: Security Forces Eliminate 13 TTP Terrorists in Multiple IBOs Across Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
item.description: Security forces of Pakistan have successfully eliminated 13 khawarij, belonging to the banned Tehreek-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) – referred to by the state as Indian proxy Fitna Al Khwarij – during five...

item.title: Tanzanian Youth Footballer Barka Selected for Catalonia U-12 Elite Squad
item.description: Tanzanian young star Barka Seif is making waves in Spain after being named in the Catalonia Football Federation's Under-12 Best XI. Playing for Damm FC, Barka is the first Tanzanian to ever make this ...

item.title: Matt Preston on cooking, hospital experiences and personal reflections
item.description: Matt Preston, the renowned food writer and former MasterChef Australia judge, recently shared his experiences on filming 'The Hospital: In the Deep End.' He revealed his amazement at observing brain s...

item.title: Nepal’s Former Rapper Balendra Shah on Track to Lead Parliament
item.description: Balendra Shah, also known as Balen, is a 35-year-old former rapper and civil engineer who is on course to become Nepal’s next prime minister. Shah first gained recognition through Nepal’s underground ...

item.title: Hydro One Supply Disruption Causes Widespread Power Outage in Eastern Toronto
item.description: On the evening of March 7, 2026, a significant power outage affected multiple neighbourhoods in Toronto, including the east end, East York, and southwest Scarborough. According to reports, the outage ...

item.title: Gulf States Deploy Layered Air-Defense Systems Against Iranian Missiles and Drones
item.description: In the past week, Gulf countries have faced multiple missile and drone attacks from Iran, prompted by US-Israeli strikes that killed Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei. Governments in the UAE, Saudi ...

item.title: Complexities of the US and Israeli strike on Iran and its impact on the Iranian people
item.description: Last week, the United States and Israel carried out a missile strike on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and several other Islamic Republic officials. The attacks have triggered further strik...

item.title: Pakistani National Killed in Dubai Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Dubai witnessed another tragic incident as a Pakistani driver lost his life in the Al Barsha area after debris from an aerial interception struck his car shortly after Iftar on Saturday evening. This ...

item.title: At least 45 attackers killed in clash with Nigerian army in Katsina
item.description: In a recent security incident in northern Nigeria, at least 45 armed assailants, described as 'bandits' by local authorities, were killed during a confrontation with the Nigerian military in Katsina s...

item.title: Zak Vyner Makes First Start for Wrexham Against Chelsea in FA Cup Fifth Round
item.description: Kenyan international Zak Vyner made his first start for Wrexham AFC in their FA Cup fifth-round clash against Chelsea on March 7, 2026. The match, played at the Racecourse Ground, saw Wrexham take an ...

item.title: Nick Koskei Laid to Rest in Bomet Amid Heartfelt Tributes
item.description: On Saturday, March 7, mourners gathered at Nick Koskei's home in Kipkebe, Silibwet Township Ward, Bomet County, to bid farewell to the young journalist who tragically died in a helicopter crash on Feb...

item.title: B.C. adopts permanent daylight saving time to improve health and productivity
item.description: On March 2, 2026, British Columbia enacted a new policy to adopt permanent daylight saving time, eliminating the semi-annual clock changes that have been in place for over a century. The policy aims t...

item.title: Lightning defeat Maple Leafs 5-2 with Kucherov reaching 100 points
item.description: In a dominant performance on Saturday, the Tampa Bay Lightning defeated the Toronto Maple Leafs 5-2, snapping a four-game losing streak that had followed an impressive 20-1-1 run. Nikita Kucherov reco...
